headline: Sabah Gas Pipeline Project Cancelled After RM3.1B Spent
context: The Sabah-Sarawak Gas Pipeline project was officially cancelled in 2025 after RM3.1 billion had been spent since 2009, with the AG's audit finding that only 14% of pipeline infrastructure was completed and much of the spending was mismanaged.

cards.big: RM3.1 billion spent on a gas pipeline that's only 14% complete — then cancelled entirely with zero asset recovery
cards.sub: Project ran for 16 years, consumed RM3.1 billion, and delivered 73km of pipeline that now sits unused in the jungle

cards.big: RM3.1 billion wasted equals 12,400 affordable homes or 6,200 rural schools that Sabah desperately needs
cards.sub: Original cost estimate of RM1.8 billion ballooned 72% to RM3.1 billion before cancellation — all sunk cost

cards.big: Rural Sabah communities promised gas supply connectivity in 2009 still rely on diesel generators costing 3x grid electricity
cards.sub: 144 villages along the pipeline route invested in land clearing and access roads based on project promises — all wasted

cards.big: AG audit found RM280 million in payments to contractors for work not performed and equipment never delivered
cards.sub: No criminal prosecution initiated despite audit findings — responsible officials transferred rather than charged

cards.big: RM3.1 billion evaporated over 16 years and nobody went to prison — in Malaysia, wasting public money isn't a crime, it's just an audit finding
Should mandatory criminal referral be triggered automatically when AG audits find payments exceeding RM10 million for undelivered work?
